title: In situ PCR using leaf epidermal peels
author: Diep R R Ganguly
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.p85dry6

steps.title: The day before
steps.instruction: The day before : prepare slides by placing frame seals on glass slide and apply pressure to borders to seal. These can be heated in thermal cycler at 90 °C for 10 minutes. Cut off excess seal to allow slide to sit appropriately in PCR machine.

steps.title: Prepare fresh fixative and keep on ice
steps.instruction: Prepare fresh fixative and keep on ice: Fixative: 63% Ethanol, 5% acetic acid. 2% formaldehyde. Prepare fresh and use within 3 hours.

steps.title: Wash 3 x 10 minutes in...
steps.instruction: Wash 3 x 10 minutes in 100 μl ice-cold Wash Buffer 1 (63% Ethanol, 5% glacial acetic acid. 4°C).

steps.title: Setup Turbo DNAse (Ambion) mastermix
steps.instruction: Setup Turbo DNAse (Ambion) mastermix. %s %s

steps.title: Setup RT mix (Thermoscript RT or Superscript III) %s %s
steps.instruction: Setup RT mix (Thermoscript RT or Superscript III) %s %s

steps.title: Bring plants to fume hood for...
steps.instruction: Bring plants to fume hood for epidermal peeling along with fixative, microscope slide and frame-seal. Keep all on ice for fixing and washing peels.

steps.title: Add 100ul fixative onto all microscope slides
steps.instruction: Add 100ul fixative onto all microscope slides.

steps.title: Make epidermal peels (try to place...
steps.instruction: Make epidermal peels (try to place 3-5 moderately sized peels per slide) and place directly into fixative on slide using a fine paint brush. Randomise which peels go to which slide (at this point, there should be no allocation of which slide will be used for which gene – can make this decision based on peel + slide quality). Wear gloves, handle formaldehyde with care.

steps.title: Incubate peels in fixative for 45...
steps.instruction: Incubate peels in fixative for 45 minutes, refreshing the fixative (with pipette) approximately every 15 minutes (apply evenly across peels on each slide).

steps.title: From this point, the remainder of...
steps.instruction: From this point, the remainder of the protocol can be performed at lab bench. Wipe off any excess liquid to ensure no formaldehyde remains. SAFE PAUSE POINT

steps.title: Wash 3 x 5 min in 1x PBS (0
steps.instruction: Wash 3 x 5 min in 1x PBS (0.1 M Na 2 HPO 4 , 1.3 M NaCl, pH 7.5, stored at room temperature <1 year).

steps.title: After last wash, remove as much wash buffer as possible
steps.instruction: After last wash, remove as much wash buffer as possible.

steps.title: Heat inactivate at 70 °C for 15 mins
steps.instruction: Heat inactivate at 70 °C for 15 mins. Place on ice. NOTE: Do not cool in thermal cycler as it can lead to cracking of glass slides.

steps.title: Add 3 μl 0
steps.instruction: Add 3 μl 0.5 M EDTA to give final concentration of 15 mM.

steps.title: Remove PBS solution and add 100...
steps.instruction: Remove PBS solution and add 100 μl of mastermix to each slide.

steps.title: Incubate 37 °C for 45 mins
steps.instruction: Incubate 37 °C for 45 mins.

steps.title: At this point, it must be...
steps.instruction: At this point, it must be decided which genes are being studied on which slides. There must be two control slides: one positive housekeeper (e.g. 18S rRNA) control and one no RT housekeeper negative control.

steps.title: Remove DNase mix and wash 2x with ice-cold sterilised water
steps.instruction: Remove DNase mix and wash 2x with ice-cold sterilised water. Do not remove final wash before RT reaction mix is ready!

steps.title: Add 98
steps.instruction: Add 98.5 µl of mastermix to all slides

steps.title: Incubate slides at 65 °C for...
steps.instruction: Incubate slides at 65 °C for 5 minutes, then place on ice for 1 min.

steps.title: Add 1
steps.instruction: Add 1.5 µl of RT enzyme (Thermoscript or Superscript III) to all slides EXCEPT negative housekeeper control. Pipette up-and-down gently to mix around.

steps.title: Run thermal cycle at 55 °C...
steps.instruction: Run thermal cycle at 55 °C for 30 mins, 70 °C for 15 mins, then hold at 4°C (better to take out of machine and keep on ice). This is a safe PAUSE POINT (keep slides covered in fridge).

steps.title: Remove RT solution from slides (keep slides on ice)
steps.instruction: Remove RT solution from slides (keep slides on ice).

steps.title: Wash slides twice for 1 min...
steps.instruction: Wash slides twice for 1 min with 100 µl ice-cold sterile water. When ready to proceed, remove as much water as possible (use a 10/20 µl pipette to get as much as possible).

steps.title: Setup PCR mix (HiFi Phusion Taq) %s %s
steps.instruction: Setup PCR mix (HiFi Phusion Taq) %s %s

steps.title: Distribute PCR mix equally across the...
steps.instruction: Distribute PCR mix equally across the slide by pipetting and run PCR: 95 °C 30 min 95 °C 10 min 60 °C 25 min 72 °C 5 min Return to step 2 20–35x 72 °C 7 minutes 10 °C hold (better to take slide out).

steps.title: Remove frame-seal chamber completely and perform remainder directly on slide
steps.instruction: Remove frame-seal chamber completely and perform remainder directly on slide. During incubation, place slide in small plastic box to minimise evaporation.

steps.title: Pipette off remaining PCR solution
steps.instruction: Pipette off remaining PCR solution.

steps.title: Wash twice for 5 min in 100 µl 1x PBS
steps.instruction: Wash twice for 5 min in 100 µl 1x PBS.

steps.title: Gently add 100 µl of 1x...
steps.instruction: Gently add 100 µl of 1x Block solution and incubate 30 min on ice. 10X Block solution : 10 mg BSA in 1 mL 10x PBS

steps.title: Dilute anti-DIG-AP antibody 1:500 in 1x...
steps.instruction: Dilute anti-DIG-AP antibody 1:500 in 1x Block solution, keep on ice.

steps.title: Pipette off as much 1X block solution as possible
steps.instruction: Pipette off as much 1X block solution as possible.

steps.title: Add 50 µl of diluted anti-DIG-AP...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µl of diluted anti-DIG-AP antibody and incubate at room temperature for 1 hour.

steps.title: Remove antibody solution and wash twice...
steps.instruction: Remove antibody solution and wash twice for 15 min at room temperature in Wash Buffer 2 (can transfer peels to another microscope slide if desired; keep in wash buffer 2). Wash Buffer 2 : 0.1 M TRIS-HCl, 0.15 M NaCl, pH 9.5

steps.title: Remove wash buffer 2
steps.instruction: Remove wash buffer 2.

steps.title: Add 50 µl BM purple (AP substrate)
steps.instruction: Add 50 µl BM purple (AP substrate). This is light sensitive, incubate in dark (e.g. drawer, or cover) and keep on ice.

steps.title: Staining can occur anywhere between 10 min – 2 hours
steps.instruction: Staining can occur anywhere between 10 min – 2 hours. Optimize as appropriate. If no signal is detected after 1 hour, refresh BM purple (take off and add another 50 µl).

steps.title: Once stain has developed, pipette off BM purple
steps.instruction: Once stain has developed, pipette off BM purple.

steps.title: Add 100 µl Wash Buffer 2...
steps.instruction: Add 100 µl Wash Buffer 2 and wash 3x 5 min to remove residual stain and debris.

steps.title: Wash once with sterile water, then remove all water
steps.instruction: Wash once with sterile water, then remove all water.

steps.title: Mount section in 40 µl 40%...
steps.instruction: Mount section in 40 µl 40% glycerol (40–150 µl depending on number of peels on slide). Do not use excessive amounts of glycerol.

steps.title: Place coverslip on top and seal corners with nail varnish
steps.instruction: Place coverslip on top and seal corners with nail varnish. Coverslip should not float (remove some glycerol if needed). NB do not slide coverslip, instead just drop directly onto peels/glycerol.

steps.title: Visualise under bright field microscope
steps.instruction: Visualise under bright field microscope. Slides can be stored at room temp and is good ~3 weeks. Ensure all peels are imaged using the same settings.
